I have Tesla's Wheel and Tire Protection Plan and noticed damage on one of my wheels.
Can you check whether the plan applies to this issue and tell me what I should do next?

affected_component: front_left_wheel
issue_category: curb_rash
observed_condition: Surface scraping and scuffing are present around the outer rim
  edge. The tire is holding pressure, and there is no reported vibration or structural
  deformation.

args.query: Tesla Wheel and Tire Protection Plan terms curb rash cosmetic damage coverage

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Wheel and Tire Protection Plan\
  \ Subscription, United States
- url: https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/tire-protection-plan-terms-and-conditions.pdf
\
  - source: Tesla, Inc.
- domain: digitalassets.tesla.com

## Content

WHEEL\
  \ AND TIRE PROTECTION PLAN SUBSCRIPTION, UNITED STATES

This agreement describes\
  \ Tesla's U.S. Wheel and Tire Protection Plan. The plan addresses qualifying failures\
  \ of Tesla-installed tires and associated wheels when the failure results from a\
  \ covered road hazard on a public road. Covered road-hazard events include physical\
  \ impacts from items such as nails, glass, rocks, debris, and potholes.

A qualifying\
  \ failure generally involves a puncture; damage that prevents a tire from sealing\
  \ to the wheel or maintaining the tire manufacturer's specified pressure; or damage\
  \ that causes vibration severe enough to impair drivability or structural integrity.
\
  
COVERAGE AND DEDUCTIBLES

The plan operates as a monthly subscription. Repair\
  \ claims for eligible covered parts have no deductible. Replacement claims are subject\
  \ to a $25 deductible for each covered replacement claim.

When a qualifying failure\
  \ occurs, Tesla first determines whether the covered part can be repaired safely.\
  \ If safe repair is not possible, Tesla may replace the covered part with a compatible\
  \ replacement.

Coverage applies only to covered parts that satisfy the agreement's\
  \ installation and eligibility requirements. Depending on when the owner subscribes,\
  \ an inspection may be required before enrollment. The agreement also contains separate\
  \ rules for eligibility, the coverage period, cancellation, transfer, and state-specific\
  \ terms.

OBTAINING SERVICE

For a qualifying failure, the subscriber should\
  \ arrange service at a Tesla Authorized Service Location within a reasonable time.\
  \ If the vehicle cannot be operated safely, the owner should stop driving as necessary\
  \ to prevent additional damage.

Tesla may require inspection before repair or\
  \ replacement. The subscriber may also need to cooperate with the investigation\
  \ of the failure and provide records related to the plan, the covered part, and\
  \ required maintenance.

Roadside Assistance is a separate service rather than\
  \ a benefit provided under this agreement. Availability, transport options, and\
  \ any related costs are governed separately.

EXCLUSIONS — WHAT IS NOT COVERED
\
  
The agreement excludes a number of damage categories and circumstances. These\
  \ include damage that existed before the plan became effective, vandalism, collision,\
  \ cosmetic damage, and certain improper or unauthorized uses and repairs.

The\
  \ agreement also excludes:

- commercial use and certain racing or off-road activity;
\
  - damage resulting from negligence, misuse, abuse, improper towing, or improper\
  \ wheel balancing;
- failures related to improper maintenance;
- tire or wheel\
  \ work performed outside a Tesla Authorized Service Location when the agreement\
  \ requires authorized service;
- wheel repair or refurbishment;
- curb rash;
\
  - vehicle mechanical failures unrelated to the covered tire or wheel;
- damage\
  \ that does not affect the operability or structural integrity of the tire or wheel;
\
  - certain total-loss, salvage, or similarly branded vehicles;
- additional damage\
  \ caused by failing to take reasonable precautions after a failure.

These exclusions\
  \ are part of the agreement's applicability boundary. A wheel or tire problem is\
  \ not covered merely because the vehicle has an active subscription. The type and\
  \ cause of the current damage still have to fall within the agreement.

COVERAGE\
  \ PERIOD

The agreement renews month to month, subject to its terms, up to the\
  \ maximum period specified by the agreement. Coverage can also end when the covered\
  \ tire reaches the stated minimum tread-depth threshold or when the agreement is\
  \ terminated.

ELIGIBILITY AND INSPECTION

Enrollment rules differ depending\
  \ on timing and vehicle condition. Some recently delivered vehicles or vehicles\
  \ with qualifying Tesla-installed tires may be eligible without an inspection. Other\
  \ vehicles may need to pass a Tesla inspection before subscription.

The inspection\
  \ requirements focus on whether the tires and wheels were purchased from and installed\
  \ by Tesla and whether the tires meet condition and tread-depth requirements. If\
  \ the vehicle does not satisfy the inspection criteria, the owner may need to complete\
  \ repairs or tire replacement before becoming eligible to subscribe.

The complete\
  \ agreement also contains liability limits, ownership-transfer rules, cancellation\
  \ provisions, arbitration terms, and state-specific requirements.
"

title: Wheel and Tire Protection Plan Subscription, United States
url: https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/tire-protection-plan-terms-and-conditions.pdf

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Tesla Vehicle Protection Plans

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/vehicle-protection-plans

  - source: Tesla

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  TESLA VEHICLE PROTECTION PLANS


  Tesla offers several optional protection products for different vehicle risks and
  ownership stages. Each plan targets a defined set of components or events rather
  than providing one universal form of coverage.


  EXTENDED SERVICE AGREEMENT


  The Extended Service Agreement is a subscription intended for eligible vehicles
  after the applicable Basic or Pre-Owned Vehicle Limited Warranty ends. It provides
  separate mechanical coverage under its own terms and conditions.


  HIGH VOLTAGE BATTERY AND DRIVE UNIT EXTENDED SERVICE AGREEMENT


  The Battery Extended Service Agreement extends protection for eligible Tesla-made
  high-voltage battery and drive-unit components beyond the original limited-warranty
  period. Covered repairs or replacements are subject to the agreement''s separate
  eligibility conditions and deductible.


  WHEEL AND TIRE PROTECTION PLAN


  The Wheel and Tire Protection Plan is a monthly subscription intended for unforeseen
  incidents affecting a vehicle''s tires and associated wheels.


  The plan is described in the context of common road hazards such as potholes, debris,
  and unexpected punctures. Depending on the current subscription terms, it can provide
  coverage for repair or replacement of qualifying tires and associated wheels for
  up to two years.


  The WTPP is a separate protection product with its own detailed terms. The general
  protection-plans page provides a high-level description rather than a complete list
  of claim conditions, exclusions, and limitations.


  WINDSHIELD PROTECTION PLAN


  The Windshield Protection Plan is a separate monthly subscription for front-windshield
  repairs and replacements. It includes its own service limits and conditions and
  is distinct from wheel-and-tire coverage.


  LUXE PACKAGE PROTECTION BENEFITS


  Certain eligible Model S, Model X, and Cyberbeast vehicles may receive a package
  that includes several protection and maintenance benefits. Depending on the vehicle
  and order timing, the package may include multi-year Wheel and Tire Protection Plan
  and Windshield Protection Plan benefits together with recommended maintenance services.


  The Luxe Package is not presented as a standalone product for separate purchase.


  CHOOSING THE RELEVANT PLAN


  Because Tesla offers multiple protection products, owners should identify which
  plan relates to the current problem and then review that plan''s specific terms.
  A broad vehicle-protection description does not by itself establish whether a particular
  wheel, tire, windshield, battery, drive-unit, or mechanical issue is covered.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Tesla Vehicle Protection Plans
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/vehicle-protection-plans

Based on the inspection, the issue is **surface scraping/scuffing around the outer rim edge of the front-left wheel**, with:

- Tire holding pressure
- No reported vibration
- No structural deformation

This appears to be **curb rash**, which Tesla’s Wheel and Tire Protection Plan specifically excludes. The plan also excludes cosmetic damage and wheel repair or refurbishment. Therefore, this issue is **not covered under the plan**.

### What to do next

1. **Avoid filing it as a covered road-hazard claim**, since it is likely to be denied under the curb-rash exclusion.
2. If the damage is only cosmetic, arrange **out-of-pocket wheel refinishing or repair** through Tesla or a reputable wheel-repair provider.
3. If you notice air loss, vibration, a bent/cracked wheel, or handling problems, stop driving if it is unsafe and schedule a Tesla inspection. Those could indicate a separate functional or structural issue that may be evaluated under the plan.
4. Keep in mind that Tesla’s terms require service for qualifying claims through a Tesla Authorized Service Location and may require an inspection.

Sources: [Tesla Wheel and Tire Protection Plan terms](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/tire-protection-plan-terms-and-conditions.pdf) and [Tesla Vehicle Protection Plans](https://www.tesla.com/support/vehicle-protection-plans).

final_answer:
Based on the inspection, the issue is **surface scraping/scuffing around the outer rim edge of the front-left wheel**, with:

- Tire holding pressure
- No reported vibration
- No structural deformation

This appears to be **curb rash**, which Tesla’s Wheel and Tire Protection Plan specifically excludes. The plan also excludes cosmetic damage and wheel repair or refurbishment. Therefore, this issue is **not covered under the plan**.

### What to do next

1. **Avoid filing it as a covered road-hazard claim**, since it is likely to be denied under the curb-rash exclusion.
2. If the damage is only cosmetic, arrange **out-of-pocket wheel refinishing or repair** through Tesla or a reputable wheel-repair provider.
3. If you notice air loss, vibration, a bent/cracked wheel, or handling problems, stop driving if it is unsafe and schedule a Tesla inspection. Those could indicate a separate functional or structural issue that may be evaluated under the plan.
4. Keep in mind that Tesla’s terms require service for qualifying claims through a Tesla Authorized Service Location and may require an inspection.

Sources: [Tesla Wheel and Tire Protection Plan terms](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/tire-protection-plan-terms-and-conditions.pdf) and [Tesla Vehicle Protection Plans](https://www.tesla.com/support/vehicle-protection-plans).

(?isx)
^
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:
      curb[\s_-]*rash
      |
      cosmetic.{0,100}(?:wheel|rim).{0,60}
      (?:damage|scrap(?:e|ing)?|scuff(?:ing)?)
    )
    .{0,300}
    (?:
      (?:is|are)?\s*(?:explicitly\s+)?exclude(?:d)?
      |
      (?:is|are)?\s*not\s+(?:covered|eligible)
      |
      (?:does|do)\s+not(?:\s+appear\s+to)?\s+(?:apply|cover)
      |
      (?:doesn't|don't)\s+(?:apply|cover)
    )
    |
    (?:
      (?:explicitly\s+)?exclude(?:s|d)?
      |
      not\s+(?:covered|eligible)
      |
      (?:does|do)\s+not(?:\s+appear\s+to)?\s+(?:apply|cover)
      |
      (?:doesn't|don't)\s+(?:apply|cover)
    )
    .{0,300}
    (?:
      curb[\s_-]*rash
      |
      cosmetic.{0,100}(?:wheel|rim)
    )
  )
)
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    out[\s_-]*of[\s_-]*pocket
    |
    (?:cash|paid|owner[\s_-]*paid)
    .{0,120}
    (?:estimate|repair|service|replacement|refinish)
    |
    (?:repair|refinish|replace|service)
    .{0,140}
    (?:
      separately
      |
      at\s+(?:your|the\s+owner'?s)\s+(?:own\s+)?(?:cost|expense)
      |
      not\s+under\s+(?:the\s+)?plan
    )
    |
    (?:ordinary|cosmetic)
    .{0,120}
    (?:repair|service|refinish)
    |
    do\s+not
    .{0,140}
    (?:file|submit|treat|assume)
    .{0,100}
    (?:claim|covered)
  )
)
.*
$
